questions.q: In which Indian state is the Sé Cathedral located?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral stands in Old Goa, in the western coastal state of Goa.

questions.q: Which European power built the Sé Cathedral?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral was raised by the Portuguese, who ruled Goa for over four centuries.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral is famous for being one of the largest churches in which region?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral is celebrated as one of the largest churches in Asia.

questions.q: Which religion is the Sé Cathedral associated with?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral is a Roman Catholic church built by the Christian Portuguese in Goa.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral is famous for housing what kind of bell?
questions.explanation: The cathedral is renowned for its 'Golden Bell', noted for its rich and resonant tone.

questions.q: In which historic settlement does the Sé Cathedral stand?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral is located in Old Goa (Velha Goa), the former capital of Portuguese India.

questions.q: On which coast of India is the Sé Cathedral situated?
questions.explanation: Goa lies on India's western coast along the Arabian Sea, where the Sé Cathedral is located.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral is dedicated to which saint?
questions.explanation: The cathedral is dedicated to St Catherine of Alexandria, on whose feast day Goa was captured.

questions.q: What type of structure is the Sé Cathedral?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral is a large Roman Catholic church built in Old Goa.

questions.q: Old Goa, where the Sé Cathedral stands, is recognised as part of which UNESCO listing?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral is part of the 'Churches and Convents of Goa', a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: In which century was construction of the Sé Cathedral begun?
questions.explanation: Construction of the Sé Cathedral began in 1562 during the late 16th century.

questions.q: During the reign of which Portuguese king did construction of the Sé Cathedral begin in 1562?
questions.explanation: Construction began in 1562 during the reign of King Dom Sebastião of Portugal.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral's exterior is primarily built in which architectural style?
questions.explanation: The cathedral's exterior is built in the Tuscan style, while its interior is largely Corinthian.

questions.q: Originally the Sé Cathedral had two towers. What happened to one of them?
options: It was struck by lightning and rebuilt
questions.explanation: One of the two original bell towers collapsed in 1776 and was never rebuilt, leaving a single tower today.

questions.q: The famous Golden Bell of the Sé Cathedral hangs in which part of the building?
questions.explanation: The Golden Bell hangs in the cathedral's single surviving bell tower.

questions.q: The capture of Goa by the Portuguese in 1510, which the cathedral commemorates, was led by which commander?
questions.explanation: Afonso de Albuquerque conquered Goa in 1510, a victory the cathedral was built to commemorate.

questions.q: Goa was captured by Albuquerque on the feast day of which saint, to whom the cathedral is dedicated?
questions.explanation: Goa fell on 25 November 1510, the feast of St Catherine of Alexandria, so the cathedral was dedicated to her.

questions.q: Roughly how long did it take from the start of construction in 1562 to the cathedral's consecration?
questions.explanation: Begun in 1562 and consecrated in 1640, the cathedral took about 80 years from start to consecration (its main body was finished by 1619).

questions.q: The interior of the Sé Cathedral features a richly gilded main altarpiece (retable) honouring which saint?
questions.explanation: The main gilded altarpiece is dedicated to St Catherine of Alexandria, the cathedral's patron saint.

questions.q: Which sea borders the coast near Old Goa where the Sé Cathedral stands?
questions.explanation: Goa lies on the western coast of India along the Arabian Sea.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral served as the seat of which church official in Portuguese India?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral was the seat of the Archbishop of Goa, who held the title Primate of the East.

questions.q: How many side chapels line the interior aisles of the Sé Cathedral?
questions.explanation: The cathedral has eight chapels, four on each side of the nave, including the famous Chapel of the Cross of Miracles.

questions.q: In which year did construction of the Sé Cathedral formally begin?
questions.explanation: Work on the Sé Cathedral began in 1562 under Portuguese rule.

questions.q: One of the cathedral's chapels holds a famous cross associated with which reported miracle?
options: The Cross of Miracles, said to have grown in size
A cross that wept tears of gold
A cross carved by St Thomas
A cross that floated on the river
questions.explanation: The Chapel of the Cross of Miracles holds a cross said to have miraculously grown larger and where apparitions of Christ were reported.

questions.q: The collapse of one of the Sé Cathedral's two towers occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: One of the two bell towers collapsed in 1776 and was never rebuilt.

questions.q: The Golden Bell of the Sé Cathedral is also historically associated with which grim institution of Old Goa?
questions.explanation: The Golden Bell was tolled during the autos-da-fé of the Goa Inquisition held nearby.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral was consecrated around which year, after its main body was completed?
questions.explanation: The cathedral was consecrated in 1640, decades after the main structure was finished in 1619.

questions.q: The Sé Cathedral is often noted as being larger than any church in which country?
questions.explanation: The Sé Cathedral is one of the largest churches in Asia and is reputed to be bigger than any church in Portugal, the colonial power that built it.

questions.q: The main facade and structure of the Sé Cathedral are built largely from which local material?
questions.explanation: Like many Goan churches, the Sé Cathedral is built mainly of locally quarried laterite stone, lime-plastered and whitewashed.

questions.q: Old Goa's churches and convents, including the Sé Cathedral, were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in which year?
questions.explanation: The Churches and Convents of Goa, including the Sé Cathedral, were inscribed by UNESCO in 1986.
